newsSinceJun6: No company-specific material news found since June 6, 2026 (no earnings, contracts, design wins, or capacity announcements). Today's +7.37% jump appears sector/flow-driven: the China semicap localization narrative (50% domestic-equipment mandate, etch/deposition substitution already >40%) plus reported passive flows into Chinese chip names from index reshuffling. AMEC's 5nm etch validation at advanced lines and share gains are pre-existing, not new this week.

sources.label: TrendForce - China domestic chip equipment adoption beats 2025 target, led by NAURA and AMEC
Investing.com - Chinese chip stocks surge as index reshuffle heralds billions in passive flows

verdicts.rankRationale: On a crowding-sensitive alpha list, a +7.4% single-day pop and +36% YTD with no new fundamental evidence is pure rerating chase - residual alpha shrinks. AMEC is also a $37B consensus China-semicap proxy, not a hidden bottleneck name, so the flow-driven surge argues for a modest demotion from rank 29.
verdicts.bull: Beijing's 50% domestic-equipment mandate and >40% etch localization keep AMEC's order book structurally underwritten; 5nm etch validation gives credible advanced-node optionality alongside compound-semi/photonics tools.
verdicts.neutral: Thesis is intact but fully recognized: localization tailwinds are consensus, and today's move is flow (mandate narrative plus index passive flows) rather than new evidence, so expected forward alpha is roughly unchanged at a worse entry.
verdicts.bear: A crowded, policy-driven multiple unwinds fast on any China stimulus disappointment or domestic price competition from NAURA/peers; US entity-list escalation could still pinch subsystems and components.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: Q2 2026 results / order disclosures confirming localization-driven etch and deposition tool orders from SMIC-led capacity buildout
verdicts.primaryRisk: Flow-driven rerating reversal in China semicap plus intensifying domestic competition compressing margins
